# Pull Request Template

## 📋 Description
<!-- Brief description of the changes -->

## 🎯 Type of Change
- [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [ ] 📚 Documentation update
- [ ] 🎨 Code style / formatting
- [ ] ♻️ Refactoring (no functional changes)
- [ ] ⚡ Performance improvement
- [ ] ✅ Test (adding or updating tests)

## 📝 Changes Made
<!-- List the main changes -->
-
-
-

## ✅ Testing
<!-- Describe how you tested your changes -->
- [ ] Unit tests passed
- [ ] Integration tests passed
- [ ] Manual testing done
- [ ] Added/updated tests for new functionality

### Test Results
<!-- Paste test results or describe manual testing -->

## 🔍 Checklist
- [ ] My code follows the style guidelines of this project
- [ ] I have performed a self-review of my own code
- [ ] I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas
- [ ] I have made corresponding changes to the documentation
- [ ] My changes generate no new warnings
- [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix is effective or that my feature works
- [ ] New and existing unit tests pass locally with my changes
- [ ] No new dependencies added (or documented in PR description)
- [ ] Security considerations addressed (no secrets, proper input validation)

## 📊 Code Coverage
<!-- If applicable, show coverage impact -->
- Current coverage: `XX%`
- New coverage: `XX%`
- Impact: `+/- XX%`

<!-- If this is a breaking change, describe the impact and migration path -->

## Reviewer Checklist
- [ ] Code is well-documented
- [ ] Tests are adequate
- [ ] No security vulnerabilities introduced
- [ ] Performance impact considered
- [ ] Documentation updated
